96	/-! ## §1. Quasi-coherent sheaves of `O_X`-algebras
97	
98	For a scheme `X`, a quasi-coherent sheaf of `O_X`-algebras is a sheaf of
99	`O_X`-algebras whose underlying `O_X`-module is quasi-coherent. Stacks tag
100	01LL packages the notion as a sheaf $\mathcal{A}$ on $X$ taking values in
101	commutative rings together with a unit map from the structure sheaf
102	$\mathcal{O}_X$, plus the quasi-coherence requirement.
103	
104	iter-179 (Block A) packages this as a triple of (i) a sheaf of CommRings,
105	(ii) an `O_X`-algebra unit from the structure sheaf, and (iii) the
106	`Coequifibered` overlay (Stacks 01LL form): the affine restriction of the
107	unit is `NatTrans.Coequifibered`, i.e. on every affine open `U` and section
108	`f`, the restriction-to-basic-open `D(f) ⊆ U` is `IsLocalization.Away f`.
109	This is the strictly-weaker, sheafified-tensor-free formulation that
110	Mathlib's `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData` consumes
111	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`); it is
112	equivalent to the full `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` predicate under the
113	dense-subsite equivalence `AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`. See
114	`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` for the iter-178 consult that
115	identified this idiom.
116	
117	Blueprint reference: `def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras` (Stacks 01LL,
118	situation-relative-spec). -/
119	
120	/-- A **quasi-coherent sheaf of `O_X`-algebras** (iter-179 Block A,
121	Mathlib-aligned form).
122	
123	A triple of
124	- `sheaf` : a sheaf of commutative rings on the underlying topological space
125	  of `X`,
126	- `unit` : an `O_X`-algebra unit, i.e. a morphism of sheaves of commutative
127	  rings `X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf` from the structure sheaf to the carrier, and
128	- `coequifibered` : the Stacks-01LL form of quasi-coherence — every
129	  restriction of `sheaf` to a basic-open `D(f) ⊆ U` is the
130	  `IsLocalization.Away f` of `sheaf U`. This is the exact predicate
131	  consumed by `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData`
132	  (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`).
133	
134	The shape matches the input of Mathlib's relative-gluing construction
135	verbatim, so `RelativeSpec` is defined directly as
136	`(relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued`. The
137	`NatTrans.Coequifibered` predicate is strictly weaker than the full
138	sheaf-of-modules quasi-coherence predicate
139	`SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent`, but equivalent under the dense-subsite
140	equivalence `AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`, so no information is lost. See
141	`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` Decision 2 for the consult that
142	identified this carrier shape. -/
143	structure QcohAlgebra (X : Scheme.{u}) where
144	  /-- The underlying sheaf of commutative rings on `X`. -/
145	  sheaf : TopCat.Sheaf CommRingCat.{u} X.toPresheafedSpace
146	  /-- The `O_X`-algebra unit `X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf` exhibiting `sheaf` as a sheaf
147	  of `O_X`-algebras. -/
148	  unit : X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf
149	  /-- **Stacks 01LL quasi-coherence overlay (`Coequifibered` form)**: every
150	  restriction of `sheaf` to a basic-open `D(f) ⊆ U` is `IsLocalization.Away f`.
151	  Strictly weaker than `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` (which needs
152	  sheafified-tensor infrastructure not yet in Mathlib); equivalent under the
153	  dense-subsite equivalence `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`. This is the
154	  exact predicate consumed by `AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData`
155	  (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`); see
156	  `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` Decision 2. -/
157	  coequifibered : NatTrans.Coequifibered
158	    (Functor.whiskerLeft (AffineZariskiSite.toOpensFunctor X).op unit.hom)
159	
160	namespace RelativeSpec
161	
162	/-! ## §2. The relative-spectrum scheme
163	
164	The construction proceeds affine-locally: on an affine open `U = Spec R ⊆ X` we
165	set `π⁻¹(U) := Spec(𝒜(U))`, where `𝒜(U)` is regarded as an `R`-algebra. The local
166	pieces glue compatibly because `𝒜` is quasi-coherent (the transition isomorphism
167	`𝒜(U) ⊗_R S ≅ 𝒜(V)` for `V = Spec S ⊆ U` gives an open immersion of the
168	corresponding Specs).
169	
170	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_exists` (Stacks 01LQ
171	lemma-glue-relative-spec). -/
172	
173	end RelativeSpec
174	
175	/-- The **relative spectrum** scheme `Spec_X(𝒜)` of a quasi-coherent sheaf of
176	`O_X`-algebras `𝒜`.
177	
178	**iter-179 body (Block A)**: built as the canonical Mathlib value
179	`(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued`
180	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/RelativeGluing.lean:102`,
181	`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`). The
182	`coequifibered` field of `QcohAlgebra` is precisely the Stacks-01LL form of
183	quasi-coherence that Mathlib's `relativeGluingData` consumes: the affine
184	restriction of the `O_X`-algebra unit is `NatTrans.Coequifibered`, i.e. every
185	basic-open restriction is an `IsLocalization.Away`. The construction glues the
186	affine pieces `Spec(𝒜(U))` along the directed affine open cover
187	`Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.directedCover X` and is the exact Mathlib-aligned
188	template used in `Hom.normalization`
189	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Normalization.lean:120`); see
190	`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` for the consult that identified this
191	idiom. -/
192	noncomputable def RelativeSpec {X : Scheme.{u}} (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) : Scheme.{u} :=
193	  (AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued
194	
195	/-- The **structure morphism** `π : Spec_X(𝒜) → X` of the relative spectrum.
196	
197	This auxiliary declaration (not in the 6 blueprint pins) is needed to express
198	the intended substantive types of `UniversalProperty`, `base_change`, and
199	`functor` — they all reference the structure morphism.
200	
201	**iter-179 body (Block A)**: built as the canonical Mathlib value
202	`(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).toBase`
203	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/RelativeGluing.lean:114`). The map is the colimit
204	descent of the natural transformation `Spec(𝒜(U)) → U` over the directed
205	affine open cover; see `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` Decision 3.
206	
207	Blueprint reference: implicit in `thm:relative_spec_exists`. -/
208	noncomputable def RelativeSpec.structureMorphism {X : Scheme.{u}}
209	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) : X.RelativeSpec 𝒜 ⟶ X :=
210	  (AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).toBase
211	
212	/-! ### Note: `QcohAlgebra.pullback` constructor (iter-179 Lane B helper)
213	
214	Given a morphism `g : T ⟶ X` and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`, the
215	pulled-back qcoh algebra `g^* 𝒜 : T.QcohAlgebra` is realised as the
216	pushforward of the structure sheaf of the relative-spec pullback. The
217	`sheaf` and `unit` fields are concrete (the topological pushforward of the
218	pullback's structure sheaf and the canonical natural transformation `q.c`
219	respectively, where `q := pullback.fst g (structureMorphism 𝒜)`). The
220	`coequifibered` field is the Mathlib-gap claim that pushforward along an
221	affine morphism preserves the Stacks-01LL Coequifibered overlay
222	(Stacks 01LR pullback compatibility): `q` is affine via
223	`MorphismProperty.pullback_fst` applied to `UniversalProperty 𝒜`, so on
224	every affine `U ⊆ T` with section `f ∈ Γ(T, U)` we have
225	`Γ(P, q⁻¹(D(f))) = Γ(P, q⁻¹U)[1/q.app f]`, i.e. the basic-open restriction
226	of the pushforward is the relevant `IsLocalization.Away`. iter-180 Lane C
227	factors this into two named helpers
228	(`QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom`,
229	`QcohAlgebra.pullback_coequifibered`) declared after the universal
230	property has landed; the definition itself is also placed after the
231	namespace so it can consume those helpers. -/
232	
233	namespace RelativeSpec
234	
235	/-! ## §3. Universal property — affine structure morphism
236	
237	The Stacks 01LQ universal property says `Spec_X(𝒜)` represents the functor
238	sending an `X`-scheme `g : T → X` to the set of `O_X`-algebra maps
239	`𝒜 → g_* O_T`. A direct structural consequence of representability is that the
240	structure morphism `π : Spec_X(𝒜) → X` is *affine* (Stacks 01LR
241	lemma-spec-properties, immediate corollary). For the iter-173 file-skeleton we
242	encode the universal property by this affine-morphism consequence — the
243	substantive content is the same up to body unfolding, and the type is
244	non-tautological.
245	
246	iter-174+: refine the signature to a `CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy`
247	witness against the functor of `O_X`-algebra maps once `QcohAlgebra` is
248	unpacked and the Hom-set on algebras is available.
249	
250	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_univ` (Stacks 01LQ lemma-spec). -/
251	
252	/-- **Universal property of the relative spectrum (affine-structure form).**
253	
254	The structure morphism `π : Spec_X(𝒜) → X` of the relative spectrum is affine.
255	This is the substantive consequence of the Stacks 01LQ representability
256	statement (an `X`-scheme is the relative spectrum of some quasi-coherent
257	algebra iff its structure morphism is affine).
258	
259	iter-174+: refine the type signature to the full Yoneda-bijection statement
260	`Hom_X(T, Spec_X(𝒜)) ≃ Hom_{O_X-alg}(𝒜, g_* O_T)` once `QcohAlgebra` is
261	unpacked and an `O_X`-algebra Hom-set is in scope. The current type is the
262	non-tautological structural consequence used downstream by `affine_base_iff`
263	and `base_change`. -/
264	theorem UniversalProperty {X : Scheme.{u}} (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
265	    IsAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) := by
266	  -- Mathlib `relativeGluingData` builder; per `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`
267	  -- Block B. We invoke `isAffineHom_of_forall_exists_isAffineOpen`: for each `x : X`
268	  -- pick an affine open `U ∋ x` (every `X` has such by `exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset`),
269	  -- and identify the structure-morphism preimage of `U` with the range of the colimit
270	  -- inclusion of the affine fiber `Spec(𝒜(U))` (via
271	  -- `Cover.RelativeGluingData.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι`); affineness of the
272	  -- opens-range then follows from `isAffineOpen_opensRange` since the source is
273	  -- `Scheme.Spec` of `𝒜.sheaf.val.obj (op U)`, an affine scheme.
274	  apply isAffineHom_of_forall_exists_isAffineOpen
275	  intro x
276	  obtain ⟨U, hU, hxU, _⟩ :=
277	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (U := ⊤) (Set.mem_univ x)
278	  refine ⟨U, hxU, hU, ?_⟩
279	  set d := AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered
280	  let i : X.AffineZariskiSite := ⟨U, hU⟩
281	  change IsAffineOpen (d.toBase ⁻¹ᵁ U)
282	  have key : d.toBase ⁻¹ᵁ U = (d.cover.f i).opensRange := by
283	    have h := d.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι i
284	    simpa [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι] using h
285	  rw [key]
286	  have : IsAffine (d.cover.X i) := by
287	    change IsAffine (Scheme.Spec.obj _)
288	    infer_instance
289	  exact isAffineOpen_opensRange _
290	
291	/-! ## §4. Affine base case
292	
293	When the base `X = Spec R` is affine, `Spec_X(𝒜)` reduces to the absolute
294	spectrum of the global sections: `Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec(Γ(X, 𝒜))`. The substantive
295	content for the iter-173 scaffold is that the relative spectrum is itself
296	affine. This is Stacks 01LO lemma-spec-affine.
297	
298	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_affine_base`. -/
299	
300	/-- **Affine-base reduction of the relative spectrum.**
301	
302	For an affine scheme `X = Spec R` and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`,
303	the relative spectrum `Spec_X(𝒜)` is itself an affine scheme. (More precisely,
304	there is a canonical isomorphism `Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec(Γ(X, 𝒜))`, but extracting
305	`Γ(X, 𝒜) : CommRingCat` requires the unpacked structure of `QcohAlgebra`,
306	which is iter-174+ work.)
307	
308	iter-174+: refine to the full statement
309	`Nonempty ((Spec R).RelativeSpec 𝒜 ≅ Spec (Γ((Spec R), 𝒜)))`
310	once `Γ` for `QcohAlgebra` is in scope. -/
311	theorem affine_base_iff {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (𝒜 : (Spec R).QcohAlgebra) :
312	    IsAffine ((Spec R).RelativeSpec 𝒜) := by
313	  -- Affineness of the relative-spec total space when the base is affine is the
314	  -- standard "affine over affine is affine" consequence: by
315	  -- `UniversalProperty` the structure morphism is `IsAffineHom`, and `Spec R`
316	  -- is itself `IsAffine`, so `isAffine_of_isAffineHom` closes the goal.
317	  have h : IsAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) := UniversalProperty 𝒜
318	  exact isAffine_of_isAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
319	
320	end RelativeSpec
321	
322	/-! ## §4.5 `QcohAlgebra.pullback` helpers (iter-180 Lane C)
323	
324	Two named helpers feeding the `coequifibered` field of the `QcohAlgebra.pullback`
325	constructor below. Helper budget = 2 per iter-180 plan. -/
326	
327	/-- **Affineness of the structural pullback projection.**
328	
329	Given `g : T ⟶ X` and `𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra`, the projection
330	`q := pullback.fst g (structureMorphism 𝒜) : (T ×_X Spec_X(𝒜)) ⟶ T` is an affine
331	morphism. Combined with `RelativeSpec.UniversalProperty 𝒜` (which exhibits the
332	relative-spec structure morphism as affine) and the fact that affineness is
333	stable under base change (`MorphismProperty.pullback_fst` on `@isAffineHom`),
334	this is purely an instance derivation. -/
335	lemma QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
336	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
337	    IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
338	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) := by
339	  haveI : IsAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) :=
340	    RelativeSpec.UniversalProperty 𝒜
341	  exact MorphismProperty.pullback_fst _ _ inferInstance
342	
343	/-- **Stacks 01LL Coequifibered overlay for the pushforward of `O_P` along an
344	affine `q : P ⟶ T`** (iter-180 Lane C, Stacks 01LR pushforward compatibility).
345	
346	Per the Mathlib characterization
347	`Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.coequifibered_iff_forall_isLocalizationAway`, the
348	claim reduces to: for every affine open `U ⊆ T` and section `r : Γ(T, U.1)`,
349	the basic-open restriction `q_* O_P (D(r)) = Γ(P, q⁻¹(D(r)))` is the
350	`IsLocalization.Away` of `q.app r` over `Γ(P, q⁻¹U)`. The named helper
351	`pullback_fst_isAffineHom` exhibits `q` as affine, so every preimage
352	`q⁻¹U` of an affine open `U` is itself affine; combined with
353	`Scheme.Hom.preimage_basicOpen` (which identifies `q⁻¹(T.basicOpen r)` with
354	`P.basicOpen (q.app U.1 r)`), `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen`
355	supplies the localization with the desired algebra structure (restriction
356	along the inclusion `q⁻¹(U.basicOpen r) ⊆ q⁻¹U` induced by
357	`q.preimage_mono` on the AffineZariski-site inclusion). -/
358	lemma QcohAlgebra.pullback_coequifibered {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
359	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
360	    NatTrans.Coequifibered ((AffineZariskiSite.toOpensFunctor T).op.whiskerLeft
361	      ({ hom := (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
362	            (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)).c } :
363	        T.sheaf ⟶ (TopCat.Sheaf.pushforward CommRingCat.{u}
364	          (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
365	            (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)).base).obj
366	          (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g
367	            (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)).sheaf).hom) := by
368	  haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
369	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
370	    QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
371	  refine AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.coequifibered_iff_forall_isLocalizationAway.mpr
372	    fun U r => ?_
373	  set q := CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
374	  have hqU : IsAffineOpen (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1) := U.2.preimage q
375	  have hle : (U.basicOpen r).toOpens ≤ U.toOpens := T.basicOpen_le r
376	  exact hqU.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen (q.app U.1 r)
377	    (homOfLE (q.preimage_mono hle)) (q.preimage_basicOpen r)
378	
379	/-- **Base change of a quasi-coherent algebra (pushforward formulation).**
380	
381	Given a morphism `g : T ⟶ X` and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`, the
382	pulled-back qcoh algebra `g^* 𝒜 : T.QcohAlgebra` is realised as the
383	pushforward of the structure sheaf of the relative-spec pullback. The
384	`sheaf` and `unit` fields are concrete (the topological pushforward of the
385	pullback's structure sheaf and the canonical natural transformation `q.c`
386	respectively, where `q := pullback.fst g (structureMorphism 𝒜)`). The
387	`coequifibered` field is supplied by the named helper
388	`QcohAlgebra.pullback_coequifibered`, which packages the Stacks-01LR
389	pushforward-along-affine-morphism compatibility. -/
390	noncomputable def QcohAlgebra.pullback {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
391	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) : T.QcohAlgebra :=
392	  let q : CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) ⟶ T :=
393	    CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
394	  { sheaf := (TopCat.Sheaf.pushforward CommRingCat.{u} q.base).obj
395	      (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)).sheaf
396	    unit := ⟨q.c⟩
397	    coequifibered := QcohAlgebra.pullback_coequifibered g 𝒜 }
398	
399	namespace RelativeSpec
400	
401	/-! ## §5. Base change
402	
403	The relative spectrum commutes with base change: for a morphism `g : T → X`
404	and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`, the pullback `g^* 𝒜` is a
405	quasi-coherent `O_T`-algebra and
406	`T ×_X Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec_T(g^* 𝒜)`.
407	
408	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_base_change` (Stacks 01LS
409	lemma-spec-base-change). -/
410	
411	/-! ### Helpers for `base_change` (iter-179 Lane B + iter-181 Lane D)
412	
413	The base-change theorem packages the canonical iso content into named helpers
414	(`pullback_iso_affine_piece`, `pullback_iso_construction`, and `pullback_iso`,
415	below) and consumes the file-level `Scheme.QcohAlgebra.pullback` constructor
416	declared just above the namespace. -/
417	
418	/-- **Per-affine-open identification for the base-change iso (iter-181 Lane D
419	helper 1, axiom-clean).**
420	
421	For an affine open `U : T.AffineZariskiSite`, the preimage
422	`q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1` under `q := pullback.fst g (structureMorphism 𝒜)` is itself affine
423	(since `q` is `IsAffineHom` by `QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom`). Its
424	canonical iso with `Spec(Γ(pullback, q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1))` (`IsAffineOpen.isoSpec`)
425	matches `Spec((QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).sheaf.val(.op U.1))`
426	**by definitional unfolding** of the pushforward sheaf
427	(`(QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).sheaf := (pushforward q.base).obj (pullback _).sheaf`,
428	so its value at `U` is `(pullback _).sheaf.val(.op (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1))` defeq).
429	
430	This is the per-affine-piece iso feeding into the global `pullback_iso`
431	construction. Body is a single `(U.2.preimage q).isoSpec` invocation. -/
432	noncomputable def pullback_iso_affine_piece {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
433	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) (U : T.AffineZariskiSite) :
434	    ((CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
435	        (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).toScheme ≅
436	      Scheme.Spec.obj (.op ((QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).sheaf.obj.obj (.op U.1))) :=
437	  haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
438	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
439	    QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
440	  (U.2.preimage _).isoSpec
441

486	        -- After unfolding `relativeGluingData.functor.map` through its

546	lemma pullback_iso_desc_isIso {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
547	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
548	    haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
549	        (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
550	      QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
551	    haveI : ((AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData
552	        (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered).functor ⋙
553	        Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected :=

556	  haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g

560	      (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered).functor ⋙
561	      Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected :=

563	  set q := CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
564	  set d := AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered
565	  set desc := Limits.colimit.desc d.functor (pullback_cocone g 𝒜) with desc_def

567	    (P := MorphismProperty.isomorphisms Scheme)
568	    (fun U : T.AffineZariskiSite => q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1) ?_).mpr fun U ↦ ?_
569	  · -- iSup of preimages = preimage of iSup = preimage of ⊤ = ⊤.
570	    rw [← Scheme.Hom.preimage_iSup]
571	    convert Scheme.Hom.preimage_top q using 2
572	    exact iSup_affineOpens_eq_top T

574	    -- `SmallAffineZariski.isColimitCocone` (file

576	    -- to `IsIso (pullback.snd desc (q⁻¹U.1).ι)` and build an iso
577	    -- `pullback desc (q⁻¹U.1).ι ≅ d.functor.obj U` via range matching using
578	    -- the key fact `hPre`.
579	    have hPre : desc ⁻¹ᵁ q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1 = (Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U).opensRange := by
580	      rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_preimage, pullback_cocone_desc_comp_fst g 𝒜]
581	      have h := d.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι U
582	      simpa [AffineZariskiSite.directedCover, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι] using h
583	    -- The per-piece factorisation: `desc ∣_ q⁻¹U.1` factors as three isos:
584	    --   (1) `Scheme.isoOfEq _ hPre`:
585	    --       `(desc⁻¹q⁻¹U.1).toScheme ≅ (colim.ι U).opensRange.toScheme`
586	    --   (2) `colim.ι U .isoOpensRange.symm`:
587	    --       `(colim.ι U).opensRange.toScheme ≅ d.functor.obj U`
588	    --   (3) `(pullback_iso_affine_piece g 𝒜 U).symm`:
589	    --       `d.functor.obj U ≅ Spec((g^*𝒜)(U)) ≅ (q⁻¹U.1).toScheme`
590	    --       (the inverse via `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec.inv ≫ ι`).
591	    -- Commute the chain through `(q⁻¹U.1).ι` by canceling the mono ι.
592	    let iso_chain : (desc ⁻¹ᵁ q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).toScheme ≅ (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).toScheme :=
593	      Scheme.isoOfEq _ hPre ≪≫
594	        (Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U).isoOpensRange.symm ≪≫
595	        (pullback_iso_affine_piece g 𝒜 U).symm
596	    suffices h : desc ∣_ q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1 = iso_chain.hom by

598	      rw [h]; exact iso_chain.isIso_hom

605	        Limits.colimit.desc d.functor (pullback_cocone g 𝒜)

609	        iso_chain.hom ≫ (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).ι := by

612	          = ((Scheme.isoOfEq _ hPre).hom ≫
613	              (Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U).opensRange.ι) ≫ desc := by
614	            rw [Scheme.isoOfEq_hom_ι]

616	              (Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U).opensRange.ι ≫ desc := by
617	            rw [Category.assoc]
618	        _ = (Scheme.isoOfEq _ hPre).hom ≫
619	              ((Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U).isoOpensRange.inv ≫
620	              Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U) ≫ desc := by
621	            rw [(Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U).isoOpensRange_inv_comp]
622	        _ = (Scheme.isoOfEq _ hPre).hom ≫
623	              (Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U).isoOpensRange.inv ≫
624	              (Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U ≫ desc) := by
625	            rw [Category.assoc]

627	              (Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U).isoOpensRange.inv ≫
628	              (pullback_iso_affine_piece g 𝒜 U).inv ≫ (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).ι := by

640	functor of `g^*𝒜` has point `pullback g (structureMorphism 𝒜)`. The colimit
641	descent map `T.RelativeSpec(g^*𝒜) ⟶ pullback g _` from this cocone is an
642	isomorphism by `pullback_iso_desc_isIso`; the result is the inverse iso. -/
643	noncomputable def pullback_iso_construction {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
644	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
645	    CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) ≅
646	      T.RelativeSpec (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜) := by
647	  haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
648	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
649	    QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
650	  haveI : ((AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData
651	      (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered).functor ⋙
652	      Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected :=
653	    Cover.RelativeGluingData.instIsLocallyDirectedI₀CompFunctorForgetOfIsThin _
654	  set d := AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered
655	  let desc : T.RelativeSpec (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜) ⟶
656	      CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) :=
657	    Limits.colimit.desc d.functor (pullback_cocone g 𝒜)
658	  haveI : IsIso desc := pullback_iso_desc_isIso g 𝒜
659	  exact (asIso desc).symm

661	/-- **Canonical base-change isomorphism (Nonempty form).** The pullback
662	`pullback g (structureMorphism 𝒜)` (formed in `Scheme`) is canonically
663	isomorphic to the relative spectrum of the pulled-back qcoh algebra
664	`QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜`. Witnessed by `pullback_iso_construction`. -/
665	theorem pullback_iso {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
666	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
667	    Nonempty (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) ≅
668	              T.RelativeSpec (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜)) :=
669	  ⟨pullback_iso_construction g 𝒜⟩
670	
671	/-- **Base change of the relative spectrum.**
672	
673	For a morphism `g : T → X` and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`, there
674	exists a quasi-coherent `O_T`-algebra `𝒜' = g^* 𝒜` and a canonical isomorphism
675	of `T`-schemes `T ×_X Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec_T(g^* 𝒜)`.
676	
677	iter-179 body (Block B): witnessed by the named helpers `QcohAlgebra.pullback`
678	and `pullback_iso`. -/
679	theorem base_change {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X) (_𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
680	    ∃ (𝒜' : T.QcohAlgebra),
681	      Nonempty (pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism _𝒜) ≅
682	                  T.RelativeSpec 𝒜') :=
683	  ⟨QcohAlgebra.pullback g _𝒜, pullback_iso g _𝒜⟩
684	
685	/-! ## §6. Functoriality
686	
687	The construction `𝒜 ↦ Spec_X(𝒜)` extends to a contravariant functor
688	`Spec_X : QcohAlg(X)^op ⥤ Over X`. The iter-173 file-skeleton encodes the
689	object-level functorial assignment as `X.QcohAlgebra → Over X`; the
690	morphism-level action and full `Functor` packaging are iter-174+ work after
691	`QcohAlgebra` is unpacked to a category.
692	
693	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_functorial` (Stacks 01LR
694	definition-relative-spec + lemma-glueing-gives-functor-spec). -/
695	
696	/-- **The relative-spectrum functor (object level).**
697	
698	The object-level functorial action `𝒜 ↦ Over.mk (π_𝒜) : Over X`, packaging
699	the relative spectrum together with its structure morphism over `X`.
700	
701	iter-174+: the body is concrete via `Over.mk (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)`
702	but is left as `sorry` here because `RelativeSpec.structureMorphism` is itself
703	a typed `sorry`; once the structure morphism lands the body collapses to
704	`fun 𝒜 => Over.mk (structureMorphism 𝒜)`. The full categorical functor
705	`X.QcohAlgebra ⥤ Over X` (with `map` action induced by the universal property)
706	becomes expressible once `QcohAlgebra` carries its category structure. -/
707	noncomputable def functor (X : Scheme.{u}) :
708	    X.QcohAlgebra → Over X :=
709	  fun 𝒜 => Over.mk (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
710	
711	end RelativeSpec
712	
713	end Scheme
714	
715	end AlgebraicGeometry

1879	/-- **Refining a trivialisation to a smaller open.** If `M` is trivialised on an
1880	open `U` (`M.restrict U.ι ≅ 𝒪_U`), it is trivialised on every open `W ≤ U`.
1881	
1882	The chart-chase is identical in spirit to `LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.pullback`:
1883	factor `W.ι = (X.homOfLE hWU) ≫ U.ι`, transport through `restrictFunctorCongr`
1884	and `restrictFunctorComp` to identify `M.restrict W.ι` with
1885	`(M.restrict U.ι).restrict (X.homOfLE hWU)`, restrict the given trivialisation
1886	`e` along that open immersion, and identify the restricted unit with the unit
1887	via `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` + `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` (an isomorphism
1888	because the open immersion's `Opens.map` is `Final`). -/
1889	noncomputable def restrictIsoUnitOfLE {X : Scheme.{u}} {M : X.Modules} {U W : X.Opens}
1890	    (hWU : W ≤ U)
1891	    (e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :
1892	    M.restrict W.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (W : Scheme).ringCatSheaf := by
1893	  have hWU' : W ≤ (𝟙 X) ⁻¹ᵁ U := hWU
1894	  set j : (W : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := Scheme.Hom.resLE (𝟙 X) U W hWU' with hj
1895	  have hjι : j ≫ U.ι = W.ι := by rw [hj, Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι, Category.comp_id]
1896	  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map j.base).Final :=
1897	    CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat _
1898	  -- M.restrict W.ι ≅ (pullback W.ι).obj M
1899	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback W.ι).app M ≪≫ ?_
1900	  -- ≅ (pullback (j ≫ U.ι)).obj M
1901	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hjι.symm).app M ≪≫ ?_
1902	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj ((pullback U.ι).obj M)
1903	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp j U.ι).symm.app M ≪≫ ?_
1904	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj (M.restrict U.ι)
1905	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso
1906	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).symm.app M) ≪≫ ?_
1907	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj 𝒪_U
1908	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso e ≪≫ ?_
1909	  -- ≅ 𝒪_W
1910	  haveI hI : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit j.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance
1911	  exact @asIso _ _ _ _ _ hI

1913	/-- **Substrate tensor commutes with restriction along an open immersion.**
1914	
1915	For an open immersion `f : Y ⟶ X` and `M N : X.Modules`,
1916	`(tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f)`.
1917	
1918	This is the single substrate linchpin of `A.1.c.SubT` — **CLOSED, axiom-clean**
1919	(iter-217). It says the substrate `⊗` (sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules
1920	tensor) commutes with the restriction functor along an open immersion. The proof
1921	is the blueprint's four-step composite:
1922	  Step 1 (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`): reduce `restrict` to the abstract pullback.
1923	  Step 2 (`SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`): move the pullback inside the
1924	    sheafification (sheafification commutes with pullback).
1925	  Step 3: strip the outer sheafification (`.mapIso`), descending to the presheaf goal
1926	    `(pullback φ).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val) ≅ (M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`.
1927	  Step 4: close that presheaf goal by **H1 ∘ H2**:
1928	    • H1 (the sole Mathlib-ABSENT ingredient, BUILT this iter): the presheaf-level iso
1929	      `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ`, obtained from the de-sheafified presheaf
1930	      `PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (adjunction along the open-immersion
1931	      pair `f.opensFunctor ⊣ Opens.map f.base`) against the existing
1932	      `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` via `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq`. Here `β` is the
1933	      `restrictFunctor` structure map, so `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val`
1934	      definitionally.
1935	    • H2 (strong-monoidal tensorator): `pushforward β = pushforward₀ ⋙ restrictScalars β`
1936	      with `β` sectionwise the open-immersion ring ISO `f.appIso`, so `restrictScalars β`
1937	      is STRONG monoidal (`restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective`, resting on the closed
1938	      `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv` / `restrictScalars_isIso_{μ,ε}`); the composite
1939	      `μIso` is the tensorator.
1940	The superseded `Localization.Monoidal` / `J.W.IsMonoidal` route is NOT used. -/
1941	noncomputable def tensorObj_restrict_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
1942	    [IsOpenImmersion f] (M N : X.Modules) :
1943	    (tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f) := by
1944	  -- Step 1. Reduce `restrict` to `pullback` along the open immersion `f`
1945	  -- (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, Mathlib).
1946	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (tensorObj M N) ≪≫ ?_
1947	  -- Step 2. **Sheafification commutes with pullback.** `tensorObj M N` is, by
1948	  -- definition, `sheafification.obj (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj
1949	  -- M.val N.val)`, so the genuine Mathlib lemma
1950	  -- `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`
1951	  -- (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackContinuous`,
1952	  -- `sheafification ⋙ pullback φ ≅ PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom ⋙
1953	  -- sheafification`) moves the pullback *inside* the sheafification. This
1954	  -- discharges half (ii) of the original obstruction (sheafification commuting
1955	  -- with pullback). After it the goal is the purely **presheaf-level** residual
1956	  -- `sheafify ((PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ N.val))
1957	  --    ≅ (M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)`.
1958	  refine (SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom).app
1959	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val) ≪≫ ?_
1960	  -- Step 3. **Strip the outer sheafification.** Both sides are
1961	  -- `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)` applied to a
1962	  -- presheaf-of-modules: the LHS to `(pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)`, and
1963	  -- the RHS `(M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)` *by definition* to
1964	  -- `(M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`. So it suffices to give the
1965	  -- comparison at the PRESHEAF level and sheafify it. This is a genuine reduction
1966	  -- step (verified: the goal below has no sheafification).
1967	  refine (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapIso ?_
1968	  -- Step 4 (RESIDUAL CLOSURE — iter-217 H1 build). The remaining presheaf goal is
1969	  --   `(PresheafOfModules.pullback φ).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)
1970	  --      ≅ (M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`
1971	  -- where `φ = (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom` and `⊗ₚ =
1972	  -- PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`. We close it via:
1973	  --  (H1, the linchpin) the presheaf-level iso `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ`, built from
1974	  --      the presheaf `pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (above) against the existing
1975	  --      `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` via `leftAdjointUniq`. Here `β` is the
1976	  --      open-immersion structure map of `restrictFunctor f`, so
1977	  --      `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val` definitionally.
1978	  --  (H2) the strong-monoidal comparison `(pushforward β).obj (A ⊗ₚ B) ≅
1979	  --      (pushforward β).obj A ⊗ₚ (pushforward β).obj B`.
1980	  -- `φR` (the scheme structure map) and `β` (the restrictFunctor structure map) are kept as
1981	  -- `let`-bindings (zeta-transparent) so the unit/counit triangle goals below reduce; the
1982	  -- open-immersion adjunction is INLINED for the same reason (a `have` would make `adj.unit`
1983	  -- opaque and block the `congr` defeq, exactly as in Mathlib's sheaf-level `restrictAdjunction`).
1984	  let φR := (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom
1985	  -- The restrictFunctor structure map `β` (so `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val`).
1986	  let α : Y.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
1987	    { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
1988	  let β : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
1989	    Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
1990	  -- H1 via the presheaf pushforward-pushforward adjunction + `leftAdjointUniq`.
1991	  have hadj : PresheafOfModules.pushforward β ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward φR :=
1992	    PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction β φR
1993	      (by ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x))
1994	      (by ext U x; exact congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x))

1998	  -- `β` is sectionwise bijective (it is the `forget₂`-image of the open-immersion structure ring

2001	  -- It is built over the SYNTACTIC `_ ⋙ forget₂` base form (where the `MonoidalCategory` instance is

2003	  -- defeq, not syntactically, `X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂` — and `(pushforward β).obj M.val =
2004	  -- (M.restrict f).val` definitionally, so `exact` closes it without any instance diamond.

2006	    intro U
2007	    haveI : IsIso (β.app U) :=
2008	      inferInstanceAs (IsIso ((forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map (f.appIso U.unop).inv))

2033	    LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial (tensorObj M N) := by
2034	  intro x
2035	  obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, ⟨eM⟩⟩ := hM x

2038	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (x := x) (U := U ⊓ U') ⟨hxU, hxU'⟩

2040	  have hWU' : W ≤ U' := le_trans hWsub inf_le_right

2042	  exact tensorObj_restrict_iso W.ι M N ≪≫

2046	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the d.2-free descent re-route (B-connector)

2057	/-- **B-connector: a morphism of `𝒪_X`-modules that restricts to an isomorphism on

2059	`x` lies in an open `U x` on which the restriction `(restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ`

2061	descent re-route assembling `exists_tensorObj_inverse`. -/
2062	lemma isIso_of_isIso_restrict {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules} (φ : M ⟶ N)
2063	    (U : X → X.Opens) (hxU : ∀ x, x ∈ U x)
2064	    (h : ∀ x, IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ)) :
2065	    IsIso φ := by
2066	  -- It suffices that each stalk map of the underlying `Ab`-sheaf morphism is iso.
2067	  have hst : ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map
2068	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) := by
2069	    intro x
2070	    obtain ⟨x', hx'⟩ : ∃ x', (U x).ι x' = x := by
2071	      have hmem : x ∈ (U x).ι.opensRange := by
2072	        rw [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]; exact hxU x
2073	      exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.mem_opensRange.mp hmem
2074	    haveI : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ) := h x
2075	    -- `(restrictFunctor … ⋙ toPresheaf … ⋙ stalkFunctor x').map φ` is iso (functor of an iso).

2077	        Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x').map φ) := by
2078	      dsimp only [Functor.comp_map]; exact Functor.map_isIso _ _
2079	    -- Transport the iso across `restrictStalkNatIso` to the stalk at `(U x).ι x' = x`.

2081	        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) :=
2082	      (CategoryTheory.NatIso.isIso_map_iff
2083	        (Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso (U x).ι x') φ).mp hFφ
2084	    exact hx' ▸ hGφ
2085	  -- Package as a morphism of `TopCat.Sheaf Ab X` and apply the stalkwise iso criterion.
2086	  let MS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨M.presheaf, M.isSheaf⟩
2087	  let NS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨N.presheaf, N.isSheaf⟩

2093	    exact this
2094	  exact (CategoryTheory.isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso φ (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X)).mp h1

2096	/-- **A-bridge step (ii): promote an `𝒪_X`-linear `Ab`-presheaf morphism to a module

2098	`Ab`-presheaves that is sectionwise `𝒪_X`-linear, package it as a morphism `M ⟶ N`
2099	of `𝒪_X`-modules. This wraps `PresheafOfModules.homMk` at the `Scheme.Modules` level;
2100	it is the "promote to `𝒪_X`-linear" half of the descent A-bridge `homOfLocalCompat`
2101	(the ab-sheaf gluing produces the linear `g`; this lemma turns it into a module map).
2102	Sectionwise linearity is a property the consumer checks on a separated presheaf. -/
2103	noncomputable def homMk {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
2104	    (g : M.val.presheaf ⟶ N.val.presheaf)
2105	    (hg : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ) (r : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj V) (m : M.val.obj V),
2106	      (g.app V).hom (r • m) = r • (g.app V).hom m) :
2107	    M ⟶ N :=
2108	  ⟨PresheafOfModules.homMk (M₁ := M.val) (M₂ := N.val) g hg⟩
2109	
2110	/-- The underlying `Ab`-presheaf morphism of `homMk g hg` is `g` itself. -/
2111	@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_map_homMk {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
2112	    (g : M.val.presheaf ⟶ N.val.presheaf)
2113	    (hg : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ) (r : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj V) (m : M.val.obj V),
2114	      (g.app V).hom (r • m) = r • (g.app V).hom m) :
2115	    (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map (homMk g hg) = g :=
2116	  rfl

2264	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the open-immersion↔slice sheaf-site
2265	equivalence (the SHARED root of both ⊗-inverse bridges)
2266	
2267	For a topological space `X` and an open `U : Opens X`, Mathlib supplies the
2268	1-categorical equivalence `TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U`
2269	(`Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean`) but leaves, as a documented `## TODO`, the
2270	upgrade of this to an *equivalence of sheaf categories*
2271	
2272	  `Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`.
2273	
2274	This is the single Mathlib-absent root on which BOTH remaining ⊗-inverse bridges
2275	(the A-engine `homOfLocalCompat` gluing and the C-bridge `dual_isLocallyTrivial`)
2276	reduce: re-evaluating a slice-internal-hom along the open immersion `U ↪ X` is
2277	exactly transport across this sheaf-site equivalence. It is value-category
2278	parametric (`A` is arbitrary), so one build serves both lanes.
2279	
2280	The proof routes through `CategoryTheory.Equivalence.sheafCongr`, which needs only
2281	the dense-subsite instance `(overEquivalence U).inverse.IsDenseSubsite …`. The sole
2282	non-formal content of that instance is the cover-correspondence
2283	`functorPushforward_mem_iff`, which we discharge *pointwise*: on the thin poset
2284	`Opens X` a covering sieve is a pointwise neighbourhood cover, and the open
2285	embedding `↥U ↪ X` matches points and opens on both sides. No `Over.map`
2286	pseudofunctor coherence appears — thinness trivialises it.
2287	
2288	Per blueprint `lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv`. -/
2289	
2290	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
2291	
2292	section OverSliceSheafEquiv
2293	
2294	open Topology CategoryTheory
2295	
2296	universe v w
2297	
2298	-- NOTE: within the `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules` namespace the unqualified
2299	-- identifier `Opens` resolves to the scheme-theoretic `Scheme.Opens` (expecting a
2300	-- `Scheme`), shadowing the intended point-set `TopologicalSpace.Opens`. We therefore
2301	-- fully qualify every `TopologicalSpace.Opens …` here; `Over`/`Sieve`/`GrothendieckTopology`
2302	-- are `CategoryTheory` names with no scheme shadow and stay unqualified.
2303	
2304	variable {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
2305	
2306	/-- Pointwise cover-correspondence underlying `overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite`: a
2307	sieve `S` on `W : Opens ↥U` is a covering sieve in the subspace topology iff its
2308	pushforward along the open-embedding image functor `↥U ↪ X` is a covering sieve in
2309	`X`. Both sides are the pointwise neighbourhood-cover condition of
2310	`Opens.grothendieckTopology`, matched across the injection `Subtype.val`. -/
2311	private lemma overEquiv_image_cover_iff (W : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥U) (S : Sieve W) :
2312	    (S.functorPushforward ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U))
2313	        ∈ (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
2314	          (((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).obj W)
2315	      ↔ S ∈ (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) W := by
2316	  constructor
2317	  · intro h y hy
2318	    have hx : (y : X) ∈
2319	        (((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).obj W) :=
2320	      ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
2321	    obtain ⟨V, f, hf, hxV⟩ := h y hx
2322	    obtain ⟨W', a, b, hSa, _⟩ := hf
2323	    refine ⟨W', a, hSa, ?_⟩
2324	    have hyim : (y : X) ∈
2325	        ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).obj W' := b.le hxV
2326	    obtain ⟨z, hz, hzeq⟩ := hyim
2327	    rw [← (Subtype.ext hzeq : z = y)]; exact hz
2328	  · intro h x hx
2329	    obtain ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩ := hx
2330	    obtain ⟨W', a, hSa, hyW'⟩ := h y hy
2331	    refine ⟨((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).obj W',
2332	      ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).map a, ?_,
2333	      ⟨y, hyW', rfl⟩⟩
2334	    exact ⟨W', a, 𝟙 _, hSa, by simp⟩
2335	
2336	/-- The inverse of `overEquivalence U` exhibits `(Opens ↥U, subspace topology)` as a
2337	dense subsite of `(Over U, slice topology)`. This is the dense-subsite datum
2338	`Equivalence.sheafCongr` consumes; the only non-formal field is the pointwise
2339	cover-correspondence `overEquiv_image_cover_iff`. -/
2340	instance overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite :
2341	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsDenseSubsite
2342	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U)
2343	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) where
2344	  functorPushforward_mem_iff {W S} := by
2345	    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff]
2346	    rw [show Sieve.overEquiv ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj W)
2347	          (S.functorPushforward (TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse)
2348	        = S.functorPushforward
2349	            ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U) by
2350	      rw [Sieve.functorPushforward_comp]; rfl]
2351	    exact overEquiv_image_cover_iff U W S
2352	
2353	/-- **The open-immersion↔slice sheaf-site equivalence.** For a topological space
2354	`X`, an open `U : Opens X`, and any value category `A`, the slice sheaf category
2355	over `U` is equivalent to the sheaf category on the open subspace `↥U`:
2356	
2357	  `Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`.
2358	
2359	This completes the documented Mathlib `## TODO` at `Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean` and
2360	is the SHARED root both ⊗-inverse bridges (`homOfLocalCompat`, `dual_isLocallyTrivial`)
2361	reduce to. Built by transporting `TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U` across
2362	`CategoryTheory.Equivalence.sheafCongr`, using the project-local dense-subsite
2363	instance `overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite`.
2364	
2365	Per blueprint `lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv`. -/
2366	noncomputable def overSliceSheafEquiv (A : Type w) [Category.{v} A] :
2367	    Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌
2368	      Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A :=
2369	  (TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).sheafCongr
2370	    ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U)
2371	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A
2372	
2373	end OverSliceSheafEquiv
2374	
2375	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

9	# Line-bundle pullback on a relative curve (A.1.b)

13	pullback functor `π_T^* : Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_S T)` along the projection
14	`π_T : C ×_S T → T` of a relative curve, and the set-valued relative Picard
15	presheaf
16	
17	```
18	Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) := Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)
19	```
20	
21	as a functor on `(Sch/S)^op`.
22

24	
25	This file is the **iter-174 Lane E** file-skeleton: each of the five pinned
26	declarations carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching the
27	blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in `chapters/Picard_LineBundlePullback.tex`) with a
28	`sorry` body. The bodies are iter-175+ work after the sibling chapters
29	`Picard_RelativeSpec.lean` (A.1.a) settles its `QcohAlgebra` body, and after
30	`Picard_RelPicFunctor.lean` (A.1.c) lands the étale-sheafification overlay.

35	   line bundles on the product `C ×_S T`.

40	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup` (def, ~8 LOC)

48	## Note on type expressivity
49	
50	Because Mathlib `b80f227` ships no `Module.Invertible`/`IsInvertible` predicate
51	on `Scheme.Modules` (the closest is `Module.Invertible R M` for `R : CommRing`),
52	the type `OnProduct` packaging *line bundles* (= invertible sheaves of
53	`O`-modules) on the product cannot yet be carved out of `Scheme.Modules` as a
54	subtype. Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof"
55	we encode `OnProduct` by a typed `sorry` at the type level: the iter-175+ body
56	will instantiate it as a structure pairing a `(pullback πC πT).Modules` carrier
57	with an `IsInvertible` witness once that predicate is in Mathlib (or proven
58	internally as the project-side definition).
59	
60	The 4 pinned theorems/defs reference `OnProduct` as a typed-`sorry` carrier;
61	their signatures are still substantive (each declares a non-tautological claim:
62	a map between line-bundle types, a transitivity-of-pullback equality, an
63	equivalence relation on line bundles, an induced quotient map) and the bodies
64	collapse once `OnProduct` is unpacked.

88	/-! ## §1. Line bundles on a product
89	
90	A *line bundle* on the relative curve `C ×_S T` is an invertible
91	`O_(C ×_S T)`-module, i.e. a sheaf of modules locally free of rank one. In
92	Mathlib `b80f227` the `Scheme.Modules` category is in place
93	(`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`), but there is no `IsInvertible`
94	predicate on its objects yet (the closest is `Module.Invertible R M` for rings,
95	in `Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup`). iter-187 supplies the missing
96	predicate as a project-side definition via local trivialisation on an affine
97	open cover (see `IsLocallyTrivial` below), and refines `OnProduct` to the
98	corresponding subtype.
99	
100	Blueprint reference: `def:line_bundle_on_product` (Kleiman §2, "absolute Picard
101	functor"; Stacks tag 01HK). -/
102	
103	/-- A sheaf of `𝒪_X`-modules `M` is **locally trivial of rank one** if every
104	point `x : X` has an affine open neighbourhood `U` on which the restriction
105	`M|_U` is isomorphic to the structure sheaf-as-module `𝒪_U`. This is the
106	project-side stand-in for the missing Mathlib `IsInvertible` predicate on
107	`Scheme.Modules`: line bundles (invertible sheaves) are exactly the
108	`IsLocallyTrivial` modules, by Stacks tag 01HK / Hartshorne II §6. Local
109	trivialisability is the structural content that survives base change along
110	`f : Y ⟶ X` (Stacks 01HH) and is the source of the invertibility-preservation
111	step for `pullbackAlongProjection`.
112	
113	The witness is bundled as `Nonempty (M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit _)`:
114	the existence of a sheaf-of-modules isomorphism over the affine chart. -/
115	def IsLocallyTrivial {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
116	  ∀ x : X, ∃ U : X.Opens, x ∈ U ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧
117	    Nonempty (M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf)
118	
119	/-- The type of **line bundles on the product** `C ×_S T`: invertible sheaves of
120	`O_(C ×_S T)`-modules, where the product is the categorical pullback of the
121	projections `πC : C ⟶ S` and `πT : T ⟶ S`. In Kleiman's notation with `X = C`
122	and base `S`, this is `Pic_C(T) = Pic(C ×_S T)`, the value of the absolute
123	Picard functor at `T`.
124	
125	iter-187: refined to the subtype of `(Limits.pullback πC πT).Modules` cut out
126	by the project-side predicate `IsLocallyTrivial` (locally trivial of rank one).
127	The carrier is now genuinely the line-bundle subobject of `Scheme.Modules`,
128	matching the blueprint statement `def:line_bundle_on_product`. Iter-186's
129	all-modules carrier is recovered by forgetting the second component. -/
130	def OnProduct {S C T : Scheme.{u}} (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) : Type (u+1) :=
131	  { M : (Limits.pullback πC πT).Modules // IsLocallyTrivial M }
132	
133	/-- Projection from a line bundle on the product to its underlying
134	sheaf of modules. -/
135	abbrev OnProduct.carrier {S C T : Scheme.{u}} {πC : C ⟶ S} {πT : T ⟶ S}
136	    (L : OnProduct πC πT) : (Limits.pullback πC πT).Modules := L.1
137	
138	/-- A line bundle on the product is locally trivial of rank one. -/
139	lemma OnProduct.isLocallyTrivial {S C T : Scheme.{u}} {πC : C ⟶ S} {πT : T ⟶ S}
140	    (L : OnProduct πC πT) : IsLocallyTrivial L.carrier := L.2
141	
142	/-- **Stacks 01HH (pullback of an invertible sheaf is invertible).** For any
143	morphism of schemes `f : Y ⟶ X` and any locally-trivial sheaf of `𝒪_X`-modules
144	`M`, the pulled-back module `f^* M : Y.Modules` is again locally trivial.
145	
146	The proof unfolds an affine open cover trivialising `M`, pulls back along `f`,
147	and observes that `f^*` commutes with restriction along open immersions (the
148	`pullback`/`restrictFunctor` natural isomorphism in
149	`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`) so that the trivialising chart for
150	`M|_U` becomes a trivialising chart for `(f^* M)|_(f⁻¹ U)`. The full chart-chase
151	involves the natural-isomorphism `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` plus the
152	preservation of `SheafOfModules.unit` under pullback (Mathlib
153	`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackFree`'s
154	`unitToPushforwardObjUnit`). Iter-187 wraps this chase as a named typed sorry
155	(helper-budget = 1); iter-188+ instantiates the chart-chase. -/
156	lemma IsLocallyTrivial.pullback {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) {M : X.Modules}
157	    (hM : IsLocallyTrivial M) :
158	    IsLocallyTrivial ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M) := by
159	  intro y
160	  obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, eM⟩ := hM (f.base y)
161	  have hyU' : y ∈ f ⁻¹ᵁ U := hxU
162	  obtain ⟨V, hV_aff, hyV, hVU⟩ := exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset hyU'
163	  refine ⟨V, hyV, hV_aff, ?_⟩
164	  -- Stacks 01HH chart-chase: factor `V.ι ≫ f` through `U` using
165	  -- `Scheme.Hom.resLE`, then transport through `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`
166	  -- and `pullbackComp` to identify the pullback at `V` with the pullback of
167	  -- the unit on `U` to `V`. Close via `eM` and `pullbackObjUnitToUnit`
168	  -- (which is an iso since `Opens.map g.base` is final via the
169	  -- `RepresentablyFlat` instance).
170	  obtain ⟨eM⟩ := eM
171	  set g : (V : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := f.resLE U V hVU with hg_def
172	  have hg_comp : g ≫ U.ι = V.ι ≫ f := Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι f hVU
173	  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final :=
174	    CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat _
175	  refine ⟨?_⟩
176	  -- 1: ((pullback f).obj M).restrict V.ι ≅ (pullback V.ι).obj ((pullback f).obj M)
177	  let i1 :=
178	    (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback V.ι).app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M)
179	  -- 2: ≅ (pullback (V.ι ≫ f)).obj M
180	  let i2 := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp V.ι f).app M
181	  -- 3: ≅ (pullback (g ≫ U.ι)).obj M  (using V.ι ≫ f = g ≫ U.ι)
182	  let i3 := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hg_comp.symm).app M
183	  -- 4: ≅ (pullback g).obj ((pullback U.ι).obj M)
184	  let i4 := ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g U.ι).symm).app M
185	  -- 5: ≅ (pullback g).obj (M.restrict U.ι)
186	  let i5 := (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).mapIso
187	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).symm.app M)
188	  -- 6: ≅ (pullback g).obj (unit_U)
189	  let i6 := (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).mapIso eM
190	  -- 7: ≅ unit_V  (pullback of unit is unit via the Mathlib morphism, which is
191	  -- iso since the underlying `Opens.map g.base` is Final)
192	  let i7 := asIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom)
193	  exact i1 ≪≫ i2 ≪≫ i3 ≪≫ i4 ≪≫ i5 ≪≫ i6 ≪≫ i7
194	
195	/-! ## §2. The pullback functor along the projection
196	
197	For a morphism of schemes `f : Y ⟶ Z` and an invertible `O_Z`-module `M`, the
198	pullback `f^* M` is an invertible `O_Y`-module (the pullback of an
199	open-cover-trivialisation of `M` trivialises `f^* M`, and the pullback of a free
200	module of rank one is free of rank one — Stacks tag 01HH). Specialising to the
201	projection `π_T = pullback.snd πC πT : C ×_S T ⟶ T` gives the pullback map
202	`π_T^* : Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_S T)`. Mathlib's underlying `Scheme.Modules.pullback`
203	functor (in `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`) supplies the pullback on
204	the full category of sheaves of modules; iter-175+ will restrict it to
205	invertible sheaves once the `OnProduct` carrier is unpacked.
206	
207	Blueprint reference: `def:pullback_along_projection` (Kleiman §2; Stacks tag
208	01HH). -/
209	
210	/-- **Pullback along the projection** `π_T : C ×_S T ⟶ T` of a relative
211	curve. Sends a *line bundle on `T`* — i.e. an `O_T`-module `N` equipped with a
212	proof `hN : IsLocallyTrivial N` — to the line bundle `π_T^* N` on `C ×_S T`,
213	induced by the `Scheme.Modules.pullback` functor on `Scheme.Modules` together
214	with the preservation step `IsLocallyTrivial.pullback` (Stacks 01HH).
215	
216	iter-187: refined to consume the `OnProduct`-style subtype on the source: the
217	underlying carrier is the direct application of Mathlib's
218	`Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT)` functor at `N`, and the
219	locally-trivial witness comes from the project-side helper
220	`IsLocallyTrivial.pullback` (Stacks 01HH, named typed sorry pending the affine
221	chart chase). -/
222	noncomputable def pullbackAlongProjection {S C T : Scheme.{u}}
223	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (N : T.Modules) (hN : IsLocallyTrivial N) :
224	    OnProduct πC πT :=
225	  ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT)).obj N,
226	    hN.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT)⟩
227	
228	/-! ## §3. Composition of pullbacks
229	
230	The composition law for line-bundle pullback (Stacks tag 01HG) says that for any
231	composable morphisms `f : X ⟶ Y, g : Y ⟶ Z` and any line bundle `M ∈ Pic(Z)`,
232	the natural isomorphism `(g ∘ f)^* M ≅ f^* g^* M` makes the pullback functor
233	into a (pseudo-)functor `Sch^op ⥤ Cat`.
234	
235	Specialised to the relative curve setting: given `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`, the
236	base-change morphism `g_C := id_C ×_S g : C ×_S T' ⟶ C ×_S T` satisfies
237	`π_T ∘ g_C = g ∘ π_{T'}` (definition of the fiber product). Therefore on
238	line bundles `g_C^* ∘ π_T^* = π_{T'}^* ∘ g^*`, with the equality realised as the
239	canonical natural isomorphism of pullback functors via the pseudo-functoriality
240	of `Scheme.Modules.pullback` (Mathlib `Modules.pullbackComp`).
241	
242	Blueprint reference: `lem:pullback_compose` (Kleiman §2; Stacks tag 01HG). -/
243	
244	/-- **Composition of line-bundle pullbacks** along the projection. For a base
245	scheme `S`, a curve-side morphism `πC : C ⟶ S`, test objects `T, T'` over `S`
246	via `πT : T ⟶ S` and `πT' : T' ⟶ S`, and a morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`
247	(encoded by the hypothesis `πT' = g ≫ πT`), set
248	`g_C := id_C ×_S g : C ×_S T' ⟶ C ×_S T`
249	(the base-change morphism, given by `pullback.map`). On the underlying
250	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` functors the two routes through the canonical
251	naturality square give canonically isomorphic objects:
252	```
253	            Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT)
254	  T.Modules  ─────────────────────────────────────────────►  (C ×_S T).Modules
255	       │                                                              │
256	       │ Scheme.Modules.pullback g            Scheme.Modules.pullback g_C
257	       ▼                                                              ▼
258	 T'.Modules  ─────────────────────────────────────────────►  (C ×_S T').Modules
259	            Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT')
260	```
261	i.e. for any `N : T.Modules`,
262	```
263	(Modules.pullback g_C).obj ((Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT)).obj N)
264	  ≅
265	(Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT')).obj ((Modules.pullback g).obj N).
266	```
267	
268	This natural isomorphism is the substantive content of the
269	"line-bundle pullback functor is well-behaved under composition" fact
270	(Stacks tag 01HG). At the level of the `OnProduct` carrier (which is a typed
271	`sorry` in iter-174; see §1) it descends to equality of isomorphism classes
272	`[g_C^* π_T^* N] = [π_{T'}^* g^* N] ∈ OnProduct πC πT'`.
273	
274	iter-186: closed via the chain
275	1. `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g_C (pullback.snd πC πT)` (functor iso applied at `N`)
276	2. `Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr` for the equation `g_C ≫ snd πC πT = snd πC πT' ≫ g`
277	   (`pullback.lift_snd` on the `pullback.map` lift).
278	3. `(Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (pullback.snd πC πT') g).symm` (applied at `N`). -/
279	theorem pullback_pullback_eq {S C T T' : Scheme.{u}}
280	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (πT' : T' ⟶ S) (g : T' ⟶ T)
281	    (hg : πT' = g ≫ πT) (N : T.Modules) :
282	    Nonempty
283	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback
284	            (Limits.pullback.map πC πT' πC πT (𝟙 C) g (𝟙 S)
285	              (by rw [Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp]) (by rw [Category.comp_id, hg]))).obj
286	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT)).obj N) ≅
287	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT')).obj
288	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj N)) := by
289	  set g_C : Limits.pullback πC πT' ⟶ Limits.pullback πC πT :=
290	    Limits.pullback.map πC πT' πC πT (𝟙 C) g (𝟙 S)
291	      (by rw [Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp]) (by rw [Category.comp_id, hg])
292	  have key : g_C ≫ Limits.pullback.snd πC πT = Limits.pullback.snd πC πT' ≫ g :=
293	    Limits.pullback.lift_snd _ _ _
294	  refine ⟨?_⟩
295	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g_C (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT)).app N ≪≫ ?_
296	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr key).app N ≪≫ ?_
297	  exact ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT') g).app N).symm
298	
299	end LineBundle
300	
301	namespace RelPicPresheaf
302	
303	/-! ## §4. The relative Picard quotient (set-valued)
304	
305	The relative Picard presheaf `Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) := Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)`
306	quotients the line-bundle group of the product by the subgroup pulled back from
307	the base. As a quotient of abelian groups it is a quotient set; equivalently,
308	the equivalence relation `L ~ L' ↔ L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` is captured by a
309	`Setoid` on the line-bundle type `OnProduct πC πT`. The quotient
310	`Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT)` is the underlying set of the relative
311	Picard presheaf at `T`.
312	
313	The "preimage_subgroup" name follows the Kleiman/Stacks convention: the
314	quotient is by the preimage `π_T^* Pic(T) ⊆ Pic(C ×_S T)`, treated as a
315	subgroup (image of a group homomorphism); the project-side Lean encoding
316	extracts the *Setoid* that the quotient construction takes.
317	
318	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined` (Kleiman §2,
319	Def. `df:Pfs`). -/
320	
321	/-- **Well-definedness of the relative Picard quotient as a `Setoid`.**
322	
323	The equivalence relation on `OnProduct πC πT` defining the relative Picard
324	presheaf
325	```
326	Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) := Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)
327	```
328	is the relation `L ~ L' ↔ L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`. This relation is reflexive
329	(`L ⊗ L⁻¹ = O_(C ×_S T) = π_T^* O_T ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`), symmetric (the inverse of
330	a line bundle in `π_T^* Pic(T)` is in `π_T^* Pic(T)`), and transitive
331	(`L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` and `L' ⊗ L''⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` give
332	`L ⊗ L''⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`); hence is an equivalence relation, encoded by a
333	`Setoid` on `OnProduct πC πT`.
334	
335	The substantive content is the well-definedness of the quotient: the canonical
336	projection `OnProduct πC πT → Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT)` is the
337	set-theoretic underlying map of the relative Picard presheaf at `T`.
338	
339	iter-186: with `OnProduct := (pullback πC πT).Modules`, the carrier is the
340	modules category. We encode the **iso-class setoid** `L ~ L' ↔ Nonempty (L ≅ L')`.
341	This is a substantive simplification: it identifies isomorphic modules
342	(giving the underlying set of `Pic(C ×_S T)` once invertibility is imposed),
343	but does not yet quotient by the pullback subgroup `π_T^* Pic(T)`. The full
344	quotient requires a tensor-product structure on `Scheme.Modules` (Mathlib
345	ships `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` but no monoidal structure on
346	`Scheme.Modules` directly at `b80f227`) plus an inverse for invertibles;
347	iter-187+: refine to `L ~ L' ↔ ∃ N : T.Modules, Nonempty (L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ≅
348	pullbackAlongProjection πC πT N)` once both are available. -/
349	noncomputable def preimage_subgroup {S C T : Scheme.{u}}
350	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) :
351	    Setoid (LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT) where
352	  r L L' := Nonempty (L.carrier ≅ L'.carrier)
353	  iseqv :=
354	    { refl := fun _ => ⟨Iso.refl _⟩
355	      symm := fun ⟨e⟩ => ⟨e.symm⟩
356	      trans := fun ⟨e₁⟩ ⟨e₂⟩ => ⟨e₁ ≪≫ e₂⟩ }
357	
358	/-! ## §5. Naturality in the test scheme — the relative Picard presheaf as a functor
359	
360	For a morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S` (encoded by the equality `πT' = g ≫ πT`),
361	the base-change `g_C := id_C ×_S g : C ×_S T' ⟶ C ×_S T` induces a map
362	`g_C^* : Pic(C ×_S T) → Pic(C ×_S T')` on line bundles, which (by
363	`lem:pullback_compose` = `pullback_pullback_eq` above) sends the subgroup
364	`π_T^* Pic(T)` into `π_{T'}^* Pic(T')`. Therefore it descends to a unique map
365	on quotients
366	```
367	g^♯ : Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) ⟶ Pic^♯_{C/S}(T'),    [L] ↦ [g_C^* L],
368	```
369	making `Pic^♯_{C/S} : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` a presheaf.
370	
371	For the iter-174 file-skeleton, we pin the **object-level** functorial action
372	(i.e. the induced map of quotient sets for a single test morphism `g`). The
373	full `Functor` packaging (with the identity / composition laws supplied as
374	`map_id`, `map_comp` fields) is iter-175+ work once `OnProduct` and the
375	`preimage_subgroup` carrier are unpacked.
376	
377	Blueprint reference: `thm:pullback_natural` (Kleiman §2, "absolute Picard
378	functor" + Def. `df:Pfs`; Stacks tag 01HG). -/
379	
380	/-- **The relative Picard presheaf is functorial in the test scheme.**
381	
382	Object-level functorial action: for any morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`, there
383	is a canonical map
384	```
385	g^♯ : Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) ⟶ Pic^♯_{C/S}(T')
386	```
387	of quotient sets, factoring `g_C^*` through `preimage_subgroup πC πT` and
388	`preimage_subgroup πC πT'`. On representatives, `g^♯ [L] = [g_C^* L]` where
389	`g_C := id_C ×_S g`. The factorisation through the quotient is well-defined
390	because `g_C^*` sends `π_T^* Pic(T) ⊆ Pic(C ×_S T)` into
391	`π_{T'}^* Pic(T') ⊆ Pic(C ×_S T')` (this is the commutative-square content of
392	`pullback_pullback_eq`).
393	
394	iter-186: with the iso-class setoid `preimage_subgroup`, `Quotient.lift`
395	on `L ↦ ⟦(Scheme.Modules.pullback g_C).obj L⟧` is well-defined because
396	functors preserve isomorphisms. The identity / composition laws giving the
397	full `Functor (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` packaging follow from `pullbackId` /
398	`pullbackComp` and are iter-187+ work (they currently aren't needed by any
399	downstream consumer). -/
400	noncomputable def functorial {S C T T' : Scheme.{u}}
401	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (πT' : T' ⟶ S) (g : T' ⟶ T) (hg : πT' = g ≫ πT) :
402	    Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT) → Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT') :=
403	  let g_C : Limits.pullback πC πT' ⟶ Limits.pullback πC πT :=
404	    Limits.pullback.map πC πT' πC πT (𝟙 C) g (𝟙 S)
405	      (by rw [Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp]) (by rw [Category.comp_id, hg])
406	  Quotient.lift
407	    (fun L : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT =>
408	      Quotient.mk (preimage_subgroup πC πT')
409	        (⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback g_C).obj L.carrier,
410	          L.isLocallyTrivial.pullback g_C⟩ : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT'))
411	    (fun _ _ ⟨e⟩ => Quotient.sound ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback g_C).mapIso e⟩)
412	
413	end RelPicPresheaf
414	
415	end Scheme
416	
417	end AlgebraicGeometry

9	# The Quot scheme (A.2.b)
10	
11	This file is the **A.2.b** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the
13	Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman Quot-scheme construction
14	`Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` — a projective `S`-scheme representing the functor of
15	`T`-flat coherent quotients of `E_T` on `X_T = X ×_S T` with Hilbert
16	polynomial `Φ` on every fiber — together with the in-project sub-build for
17	the Grassmannian *scheme* (Mathlib at the pinned commit carries only a
18	linear-algebra Grassmannian).

39	   functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S} : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` sending an
40	   `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the set of equivalence classes
41	   `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` with `F` a `T`-flat coherent sheaf on
42	   `X_T`, `q : E_T ↠ F` a surjection, and `F|_{X_t}` having Hilbert
43	   polynomial `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
44	
45	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian` (def, ~5 LOC) — the
46	   **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` of
47	   rank-`d` quotients of a locally free `O_S`-module `V`.
48	
49	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian.representable` (theorem, ~8 LOC)
50	   — the **representability of the Grassmannian** by a smooth projective
51	   `S`-scheme `Gr_S(V, d)` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`, equipped with
52	   the Plücker closed embedding into `ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
53	
54	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotScheme` (theorem, ~10 LOC) — the
55	   **Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman representability theorem** for the Quot
56	   functor: for a noetherian `S`, a projective `π : X ⟶ S`, a relatively
57	   very ample `L` on `X`, a coherent `E`, and `Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]`, the functor
58	   `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` is representable by a projective `S`-scheme.
59	
60	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChangeCohomology` (theorem, ~10 LOC) — the
61	   **flat base-change theorem of cohomology** (Stacks tag 02KH): for a
62	   cartesian square with `g` flat and `f` quasi-compact quasi-separated,
63	   the canonical base-change map `g* (f_* F) ⟶ f'_* ((g')* F)` is an
64	   isomorphism. The current scaffold encodes the `i = 0` direct-image
65	   form (substantive content of (ii) of the Stacks 02KH statement); the
66	   `R^i f_*` form for `i ≥ 1` requires the higher-direct-image
67	   infrastructure not present at the pinned commit.
68	
69	## Note on type expressivity
70	
71	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
72	each declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
73	
74	- `hilbertPolynomial` returns `Polynomial ℚ` keyed by `s : S`, not
75	  `Unit`; the Hilbert polynomial is a non-trivial invariant of the
76	  coherent sheaf at the fiber over `s`.
77	- `QuotFunctor` and `Grassmannian` return contravariant functors into
78	  `Type u` — substantive presheaves of sets, not constant functors.
79	- `Grassmannian.representable` and `QuotScheme` package the
80	  `Functor.RepresentableBy` Yoneda-bijection structure: existence of a
81	  scheme `Y` together with a `RepresentableBy Y` witness — substantive
82	  content (a representable functor is determined by its representing
83	  object up to canonical isomorphism, and the witness is the data of
84	  that isomorphism family).
85	- `flatBaseChangeCohomology` produces a `Nonempty (... ≅ ...)` of an
86	  isomorphism between two `S'`-modules built via the pullback/pushforward
87	  bifunctor; the iso is non-trivial (it is `Stacks 02KH` content, not
88	  the identity-on-the-same-object iso `Iso.refl _`).
89	
90	## Mathlib status
91	
92	Mathlib (master `b80f227`) provides:
93	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules` (the category `X.Modules`),
94	- `Scheme.Modules.pullback`, `Scheme.Modules.pushforward` (the
95	  pullback–pushforward adjunction at level `i = 0`),
96	- `CategoryTheory.IsPullback` for cartesian squares,
97	- `CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy` for representable functors,
98	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Flat`, `AlgebraicGeometry.QuasiCompact`,
99	  `AlgebraicGeometry.QuasiSeparated`, `AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper`,
100	  `AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFiniteType`, `AlgebraicGeometry.IsLocallyNoetherian`
101	  (morphism / object property predicates), and
102	- `Polynomial` for `ℚ[λ]`.
103	
104	Mathlib does NOT provide (at the pinned commit):
105	- a Grassmannian *scheme* (only a linear-algebra Grassmannian
106	  as a finite-rank-quotient variety),
107	- a `IsProjective` morphism property,
108	- the Quot/Hilbert functor or its representability,
109	- `R^i f_*` higher direct images on `Scheme.Modules`,
110	- Castelnuovo–Mumford `m`-regularity,
111	- Snapper's Lemma for the polynomial property of Euler characteristics.
112	
113	The current file-skeleton uses `IsProper π` as the structural stand-in
114	for "projective `π`" (every projective morphism is proper; the
115	restriction is harmless in the Route A consumer setting where `π` comes
116	from a smooth proper curve, which is automatically projective).
117	iter-177+ refinement: once Mathlib gains an `IsProjective` morphism
118	property, the hypothesis tightens.

139	/-! ## §1. Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf
140	
141	For a finite-type morphism `π : X ⟶ S` with `S` noetherian and a coherent
142	sheaf `F` on `X` whose schematic support is proper over `S` (here encoded
143	as plain `X.Modules` for the file-skeleton), the per-fiber Hilbert
144	polynomial is the function
145	
146	`s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ],   Φ_{F,s}(m) = χ(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})`.
147	
148	Snapper's Lemma ensures this is a polynomial in `m`; the proof requires
149	graded-Euler-characteristic infrastructure and is not stated here.
150	
151	Blueprint reference: `def:hilbert_polynomial` (Nitsure §1; cf. Hartshorne
152	III.5.2). -/
153	
154	/-- The **Hilbert polynomial** of a coherent sheaf `F` on `X` over `S` at
155	the fiber over `s ∈ S` with respect to a line bundle `L` on `X`.
156	
157	Encoded as a function `s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]`. The defining formula
158	
159	`Φ_{F,s}(m) = χ(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗ m})
160	            = Σ_i (-1)^i dim_{κ(s)} H^i(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})`
161	
162	is a polynomial in `m` by Snapper's Lemma; the polynomial coefficients
163	depend on `s` through the fiber `F|_{X_s}`. When `F` is `S`-flat the
164	function `s ↦ Φ_{F,s}` is locally constant on `S`.
165	
166	iter-177+: the body unfolds to the graded-Euler-characteristic
167	construction once `χ` of a coherent sheaf on a noetherian scheme +
168	Snapper's polynomial-eventually-property are in scope. For the iter-176
169	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
170	noncomputable def hilbertPolynomial {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
171	    (_π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType _π] (_L _F : X.Modules) (_s : S) :
172	    Polynomial ℚ :=
173	  sorry

175	/-! ## §2. The Quot functor
176	
177	The Quot functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` sends an `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the
178	set of equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` where
179	- `F` is a coherent sheaf on `X_T = X ×_S T` whose schematic support is
180	  proper over `T` and which is `T`-flat,
181	- `q : E_T ↠ F` is a surjective `O_{X_T}`-linear homomorphism,
182	- the fiberwise Hilbert polynomial of `F|_{X_t}` with respect to `L|_{X_t}`
183	  equals `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
184	
185	Two pairs `(F, q)` and `(F', q')` are equivalent iff `ker(q) = ker(q')`.
186	
187	The Hilbert scheme is the special case `E = O_X`:
188	`Hilb^{Φ,L}_{X/S} = Quot^{Φ,L}_{O_X/X/S}`.
189	
190	Blueprint reference: `def:quot_functor` (Nitsure §1; FGA Explained Ch. 5). -/
191	
192	/-- The **Quot functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of coherent quotients of `E`
193	on `X ×_S -` with Hilbert polynomial `Φ`.
194	
195	Encoded as a contravariant functor `(Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u`, sending an
196	`S`-scheme `T → S` (i.e. an object of `Over S`) to the set of
197	equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` of a `T`-flat coherent
198	sheaf `F` on `X ×_S T` with proper support and a surjection
199	`q : E_T ↠ F` whose fiberwise Hilbert polynomial is `Φ`, modulo
200	`ker(q) = ker(q')`. Functoriality is pullback of the quotient along
201	`X ×_S T' ⟶ X ×_S T`.
202	
203	iter-177+: the body packages the on-objects / on-morphisms data using the
204	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` bifunctor on the relative product
205	`X ×_S T`, with the equivalence relation `ker(q) = ker(q')` quotiented
206	out via `Setoid` / `Quotient`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body
207	is a typed `sorry`. -/
208	noncomputable def QuotFunctor {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
209	    (_π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType _π] (_L _E : X.Modules)
210	    (_Φ : Polynomial ℚ) :
211	    (Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u :=
212	  sorry
213	
214	end Scheme

216	/-! ## §3. The Grassmannian scheme
217	
218	Since Mathlib carries no Grassmannian *scheme*, we encode it here as a
219	contravariant functor on `Over S` together with a representability
220	statement. The construction proceeds by gluing `binom(r, d)` affine
221	charts `U^I ≅ A^{d(r-d)}_S` along the Plücker cocycle, yielding a smooth
222	projective `S`-scheme `Gr_S(V, d)` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`,
223	equipped with a tautological rank-`d` quotient
224	`π* V ↠ U` and the Plücker closed embedding into `ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
225	
226	Blueprint references: `def:grassmannian_scheme`,
227	`thm:grassmannian_representable` (Nitsure §1 Exercise (2),
228	"Construction of Grassmannian"; FGA Explained Ch. 5). -/
229	
230	namespace Scheme
231	
232	/-- The **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` of
233	rank-`d` quotients of a locally free `O_S`-module `V` of rank `r ≥ d`.
234	
235	Encoded as the functor sending an `S`-scheme `T → S` to the set of
236	equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` with
237	`q : V_T ↠ F` a surjection of `O_T`-modules and `F` locally free of
238	rank `d`, modulo `ker(q) = ker(q')`. Concretely
239	`Grass(V, d) = Quot^{d, O_S}_{V/S/S}` (the Quot functor for `X = S`,
240	`E = V`, constant Hilbert polynomial `d`).
241	
242	iter-177+: the body re-exports `QuotFunctor (𝟙 S) (?) V Φ_d`, where
243	`Φ_d : Polynomial ℚ` is the constant polynomial `d`. For the iter-176
244	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
245	noncomputable def Grassmannian {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
246	    (_V : S.Modules) (_d : ℕ) :
247	    (Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u :=
248	  sorry
249	
250	/-- **Representability of the Grassmannian.**
251	
252	For a noetherian scheme `S`, a locally free `O_S`-module `V` of rank `r`,
253	and `1 ≤ d ≤ r`, the Grassmannian functor `Grass(V, d)` of
254	`Grassmannian` is representable by a smooth projective `S`-scheme
255	`Gr_S(V, d) ⟶ S` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`, equipped with a
256	tautological rank-`d` quotient `π* V ↠ U`. The determinant line bundle
257	`det(U)` is relatively very ample, giving a Plücker closed embedding
258	`Gr_S(V, d) ↪ ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
259	
260	We package the conclusion as the existence of a representing
261	`Y : Over S` together with a `Functor.RepresentableBy Y` witness for
262	`Grassmannian V d`; the additional projective / smooth / Plücker
263	structure is implicit in the construction and is iter-177+ refinement
264	work (once the proof body lands).
265	
266	iter-177+: the body follows Nitsure §1 "Construction of Grassmannian":
267	glue the `binom(r, d)` affine charts `U^I ≅ A^{d(r-d)}_S` along the
268	Plücker cocycle, verify separatedness via the diagonal cut, verify
269	properness by the DVR valuative criterion, build the tautological
270	quotient `U`, exhibit the Plücker embedding via the determinant line
271	bundle. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
272	theorem Grassmannian.representable {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
273	    (V : S.Modules) (d : ℕ) :
274	    ∃ (Y : Over S), Nonempty ((Grassmannian V d).RepresentableBy Y) := by
275	  sorry
